Interface IToolTipControllerQueryData

Provides data for the QueryObjectInfo event. event.

Namespace: PhiRobotics.UI.ToolTips
Assembly: PhiRobotics.UI.dll
Syntax
public interface IToolTipControllerQueryData
Remarks

This data object allows event handlers to inspect and modify the tooltip information before it is displayed.

The instance of IToolTipControlInfo is provided via the Info property and can be modified or replaced by event handlers.

Properties

ControlMousePosition

Gets the cursor position in client coordinates relative to SelectedControl.

Declaration
Point ControlMousePosition { get; }
Property Value
Type Description
Point

A Point structure that specifies the position of the mouse cursor relative to the SelectedControl's upper left corner.

Info

Gets or sets info that contains the object which uniquely identifies the visual element at the current position.

Declaration
IToolTipControlInfo Info { get; set; }
Property Value
Type Description
IToolTipControlInfo

An instance of IToolTipControlInfo that provides the information of the tooltip that is to be displayed.

SelectedControl

Gets or sets the control that raised the tooltip query event.

Declaration
Control SelectedControl { get; set; }
Property Value
Type Description
Control

A Control﻿ object for which the event is fired.
Remarks

Some complex controls can display different tooltips for each control element. In this case, use the SelectedControl property to access the control and use the SelectedObject property to obtain the control’s element that invoked the tooltip.

SelectedObject

Gets or sets the specific visual element within the control for which the tooltip is requested.

Declaration
object SelectedObject { get; set; }
Property Value
Type Description
object

An object defining the element for which to display the tooltip.
Remarks
Represents the specific visual element within the control for which the tooltip is requested (for example, a grid cell, node, or custom-drawn region). Some complex controls can display different tooltips for each control element. In this case, use the SelectedControl property to access the control and use the SelectedObject property to obtain the control’s element that invoked the tooltip.

See Also

QueryObjectInfo
